Survey-Data-Analysis

Alibi Shokputov

2026-03-04

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(scales)
library(tidyverse)
library(survey)
library(nnet)
library(sandwich)
library(lmtest)
library(patchwork)
library(ggrepel)
library(tidyr)
library(srvyr)
library(forcats)
df <- read_csv('data/Final_Data_Paper2/final_sample_cleaned.csv')

Part 1: Descriptive trends

p_stars <- function(p) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    p < 0.001 ~ "***",
    p < 0.01  ~ "**",
    p < 0.05  ~ "*",
    p < 0.10  ~ "\u2020",
    TRUE ~ ""
  )
}

theme_publication <- function(base_size = 20) {
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size),
      axis.text = element_text(size = base_size - 1),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size - 1),
      legend.text = element_text(size = base_size - 2),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size + 2, hjust = 0),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = base_size, hjust = 0, color = "gray40"),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size),
      plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = base_size - 3),
      plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
    )
}
trend_teleworkable_data <- df %>%
  filter(SOC_Teleworkable_Final==0) %>%
  group_by(SurveyYear, work_remote_clean_1) %>%
  summarise(weighted_n = sum(w_trim), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(SurveyYear) %>%
  mutate(
    total = sum(weighted_n),
    pct = weighted_n / total * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    arrangement_label = factor(
      work_remote_clean_1,
      levels = c("In-Person", "Hybrid", "Remote"),
      labels = c("In-Person", "Hybrid", "Remote")
    )
  )

ggplot(trend_teleworkable_data, 
       aes(x = SurveyYear, 
                       y = pct, 
                       color = arrangement_label, 
                       group = arrangement_label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_text_repel(
    aes(label = paste0(round(pct, 0), "%")),
    size = 8,
    fontface = "bold",
    show.legend = FALSE,
    direction = "y",
    nudge_y = -0.5,
    box.padding = 0.75,
    point.padding = 0.3,
    segment.color = NA,
    max.overlaps = Inf
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "In-Person" = "#2C5F8A",
      "Hybrid" = "#E8973A",
      "Remote" = "#4A9B6E"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(2023, 2024, 2025),
    labels = c("2023", "2024", "2025")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Work Arrangements among Non-Teleworkable Workers, 2023–2025",
    subtitle = "Employed Maryland Adults (Weighted Percentages)",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    color = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Maryland Commuter Survey, 2023–2025.\nInfrequent hybrid workers (2025) reclassified as in-person. Multiple worksite workers (2023–2024) reclassified as in-person.\nWeighted using post-stratification weights calibrated to 2020-2024 ACS 5-year estimates."
  ) +
  theme_publication()

ggsave(
      'results/work_arr_dist_fig_nonteleworkable.png',
      width = 15,
      height = 10,
      dpi = 300
    )
teleworkable_by_arr <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(SOC_Teleworkable_Final)) %>%
  mutate(
    arrangement = factor(work_remote_clean_1, 
                         levels = c("In-Person", "Hybrid", "Remote")),
    teleworkable_label = case_when(
      SOC_Teleworkable_Final == 1 ~ 'Teleworkable',
      SOC_Teleworkable_Final == 0 ~ 'Non-Teleworkable'
    )
  )

teleworkable_pct <- teleworkable_by_arr %>%
  group_by(arrangement, teleworkable_label) %>%
  summarise(
    wt_n = sum(w_trim),
    unwt_n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(arrangement) %>%
  mutate(pct = wt_n / sum(wt_n) * 100) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(teleworkable_pct,
                 aes(x = arrangement, 
                     y = pct, 
                     fill = teleworkable_label)) +
  geom_col(position = "stack", width = 0.65) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(pct >= 3, paste0(round(pct, 0), "%"), "")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.55),
    size = 10, fontface = "bold", color = "white"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(pct >= 3, paste0("(n= ", scales::comma(unwt_n), ")"))),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.35),
    lineheight = 0.8, 
    size = 8, color = "white"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    'Teleworkable' = "#009E73",
    'Non-Teleworkable' = "#D55E00"
    
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.02))
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  labs(
    title = "Work Arrangements by Dingel-Neiman Occupational Teleworkability",
    subtitle = "Employed Maryland Adults (Weighted Percentages)",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    color = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Maryland Commuter Survey, 2023–2025.\nTotal sample includes 2,550 workers, of whom 159 have missing teleworkability data.\nUnweighted counts (n) shown in parentheses. \nWeighted using post-stratification weights calibrated to 2020–2024 ACS 5-year estimates."
  ) +
  theme_publication() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )

ggsave(
      'results/work_arr_teleworkable_crosstab.png',
      width = 15,
      height = 10,
      dpi = 300
    )

Part 2: Multinomial Logit Model

1. DATA ASSEMBLY

m1_dat <- df %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(w_trim)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    arrangement = factor(work_remote_clean_1, levels = c("In-Person", "Hybrid", "Remote")),
    teleworkable = factor(ifelse(SOC_Teleworkable_Final == 1, "Teleworkable", "Not Teleworkable"), levels = c("Not Teleworkable", "Teleworkable")),
    # office_setting = factor(office_based, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Non-Office", "Office")),
    industry = factor(naics_industry_collapsed, levels = c(
      "Retail/Food/Hospitality", "Professional/Finance/Information", "Health Care",
      "Education", "Government", "Construction/Manufacturing/Transport", "Other Industries")),
    income = case_when(
      income_clean %in% c("01_Under_20k", "02_20k_35k", "03_35k_50k") ~ "<$50k",
      income_clean %in% c("04_50k_75k", "05_75k_100k") ~ "$50-100k",
      income_clean == "06_100k_150k" ~ "$100-150k",
      income_clean == "07_Over_150k" ~ ">$150k"
    ),
    income = factor(income, levels = c("$50-100k", "<$50k", "$100-150k", ">$150k")),
    age = case_when(
      age_clean %in% c("18-24", "25-34") ~ "18-34",
      age_clean %in% c("35-44", "45-54") ~ "35-54",
      age_clean %in% c("55-64", "65_plus") ~ "55+"
    ),
    age = factor(age, levels = c("35-54", "18-34", "55+")),
    education = case_when(
      education_clean == "pre_bachelor" ~ "Pre-bachelor",
      education_clean == "bachelor_degree" ~ "Bachelor's",
      education_clean == "graduate_degree" ~ "Graduate"
    ),
    education = factor(education, levels = c("Bachelor's", "Pre-bachelor", "Graduate")),
    employment = case_when(
      work_status_clean == "full_time" ~ "Full-time",
      TRUE ~ "Not full-time"
    ),
    employment = factor(employment, levels = c("Full-time", "Not full-time")),
    gender = factor(gender_clean, levels = c("Male", "Female")),
    race = case_when(
      race_clean == "white" ~ "White",
      race_clean == "black" ~ "Black",
      race_clean == "hispanic_latinx" ~ "Hispanic/Latinx",
      race_clean %in% c("asian", "multi_racial_other") ~ "Other"
    ),
    race = factor(race, levels = c("White", "Black", "Hispanic/Latinx", "Other")),
    household = factor(household_lifecycle, levels = c("Adults-Shared", "Solitary", "Family")),
    vehicles = case_when(
      hh_vehicles_clean %in% c("0_Car_Free", "1_Vehicle") ~ "0-1",
      hh_vehicles_clean %in% c("2_Vehicles", "3_Plus_Vehicles") ~ "2+"
    ),
    vehicles = factor(vehicles, levels = c("0-1", "2+")),
    county_type = factor(
      ifelse(home_central_outlying_county == "Central", "Central", "Outlying"),
      levels = c("Central", "Outlying")
    ),
    home_cbsa = case_when(
      home_cbsa_title == "Baltimore-Columbia-Towson, MD" ~ "Baltimore",
      home_cbsa_title == "Washington-Arlington-Alexandria, DC-VA-MD-WV" ~ "Washington",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ),
    home_cbsa = factor(home_cbsa, levels = c("Baltimore", "Washington", "Other")),
    year = factor(SurveyYear, levels = c(2023, 2024, 2025)),
    county = as.factor(home_county)
  ) %>%
  drop_na(arrangement, teleworkable, industry, income, age, education,
          employment, gender, race, household, vehicles, county_type,
          home_cbsa, year)

2. CLUSTER-ROBUST VARIANCE FOR MULTINOMIAL LOGIT

vcov_multinom_cluster <- function(model, cluster, type = c("CR0", "CR1")) {
  type <- match.arg(type)
  mf <- model.frame(model)
  X  <- model.matrix(model)
  y  <- model.response(mf)
  y  <- droplevels(factor(y))
  P  <- fitted(model)
  
  if (is.null(dim(P))) stop("fitted(model) did not return an n x J probability matrix.")
  
  lev <- levels(y)
  if (is.null(colnames(P))) colnames(P) <- lev
  P <- P[, lev, drop = FALSE]
  Y <- stats::model.matrix(~ y - 1)
  colnames(Y) <- lev
  
  n <- nrow(X)
  if (length(cluster) != n) stop("cluster length must match estimation sample size.")
  cl <- droplevels(factor(cluster))
  if (anyNA(cl)) stop("cluster has NA values.")
  
  eq_names <- lev[-1]
  S_list <- vector("list", length(eq_names))
  for (k in seq_along(eq_names)) {
    j <- k + 1
    Sk <- X * as.numeric(Y[, j] - P[, j])
    colnames(Sk) <- paste0(eq_names[k], ":", colnames(X))
    S_list[[k]] <- Sk
  }
  S <- do.call(cbind, S_list)
  U <- rowsum(S, cl, reorder = FALSE)
  meat <- crossprod(U)
  bread <- vcov(model)
  
  bnames <- colnames(bread)
  idx <- match(bnames, colnames(S))
  if (anyNA(idx)) stop("Name mismatch between vcov(model) and score columns.")
  meat <- meat[idx, idx, drop = FALSE]
  
  V <- bread %*% meat %*% bread
  
  if (type == "CR1") {
    G <- nlevels(cl)
    K <- ncol(S)
    adj <- (G / (G - 1)) * ((n - 1) / (n - K))
    V <- adj * V
  }
  V
}

3. TIDY ODDS RATIOS WITH CLUSTERED SEs

tidy_multinom_or <- function(model, V, df_t, conf_level = 0.95) {
  alpha <- 1 - conf_level
  crit <- qt(1 - alpha / 2, df = df_t)
  
  b <- coef(model)
  if (is.vector(b)) b <- matrix(b, nrow = 1, dimnames = list(names(b), names(b)))
  
  outcomes <- rownames(b)
  terms <- colnames(b)
  
  long <- expand.grid(outcome = outcomes, term = terms, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  long$estimate <- mapply(function(o, t) b[o, t], long$outcome, long$term)
  long$param <- paste0(long$outcome, ":", long$term)
  
  se_all <- sqrt(diag(V))
  pos <- match(long$param, names(se_all))
  if (anyNA(pos)) stop("Could not match some coefficients to vcov names.")
  
  long$se <- se_all[pos]
  long$t  <- long$estimate / long$se
  long$p  <- 2 * pt(abs(long$t), df = df_t, lower.tail = FALSE)
  long$OR    <- exp(long$estimate)
  long$OR_lo <- exp(long$estimate - crit * long$se)
  long$OR_hi <- exp(long$estimate + crit * long$se)
  
  long %>% dplyr::select(outcome, term, estimate, se, t, p, OR, OR_lo, OR_hi)
}

4. MODEL FIT STATISTICS

m1_fit_tbl <- function(model, data_used, outcome_var) {
  ll  <- as.numeric(logLik(model))
  aic <- AIC(model)
  bic <- BIC(model)
  n   <- nrow(data_used)
  
  pred <- predict(model, type = "class")
  obs  <- data_used[[outcome_var]]
  acc  <- mean(pred == obs)
  
  cm <- table(obs, pred)
  recall <- diag(prop.table(cm, 1))
  bal_acc <- mean(recall, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  tibble(
    N = n,
    Counties = n_distinct(data_used$county),
    logLik = round(ll, 1),
    AIC = round(aic, 1),
    BIC = round(bic, 1),
    Accuracy = round(acc, 3),
    BalancedAccuracy = round(bal_acc, 3)
  )
}

5. PREPARE PLOT DATA

prep_or_plot <- function(tidy_df,
                         terms_to_plot,
                         term_labels,
                         term_order,
                         outcomes = c("Hybrid", "Remote"),
                         p_label_cutoff = 0.10,
                         drop_intercept = TRUE,
                         drop_separation = TRUE) {
  
  df <- tidy_df %>%
    { if (drop_intercept) filter(., term != "(Intercept)") else . } %>%
    filter(outcome %in% outcomes)
  
  if (!is.null(terms_to_plot)) {
    df <- df %>% filter(term %in% terms_to_plot)
  }
  
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      term_label = dplyr::recode(term, !!!term_labels),
      outcome = factor(outcome, levels = outcomes),
      label_text = if_else(p < p_label_cutoff,
                           paste0(sprintf("%.2f", OR), p_stars(p)),
                           ""),
      font_face = if_else(p < 0.05, "bold", "plain")
    )
  
  df <- df %>%
    group_by(term) %>%
    mutate(x_lab = max(OR_hi, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.08) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  if (!is.null(term_order)) {
    df <- df %>%
      mutate(term_label = factor(term_label, levels = rev(term_order)))
  }
  
  df %>% droplevels()
}

6. PLOT ODDS RATIOS

plot_or <- function(plot_df,
                    title = "Correlates of Work Arrangement",
                    subtitle = "Odds Ratios with County-Clustered 95% CIs (Log Scale).\nReference: In-Person Worker.",
                    caption = "",
                    x_breaks = c(0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10),
                    x_limits = c(0.15, 15),
                    dodge_width = 0.7,
                    point_size = 3.5,
                    label_size = 7) {
  
  pos <- position_dodge(width = dodge_width)
  
  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = OR, y = term_label, color = outcome, shape = outcome)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_errorbarh(
      aes(xmin = OR_lo, xmax = OR_hi),
      height = 0.2, position = pos, alpha = 0.85, linewidth = 1.1
    ) +
    geom_point(position = pos, size = point_size, alpha = 0.95) +
    geom_text(
      aes(x = x_lab, label = label_text, fontface = font_face),
      position = pos, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 0, size = label_size,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_x_log10(breaks = x_breaks, labels = as.character(x_breaks), limits = x_limits) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Hybrid" = "#E69F00", "Remote" = "#0072B2")) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("Hybrid" = 15, "Remote" = 16)) +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
    labs(
      title = title,
      subtitle = subtitle,
      x = "Odds Ratio (Log Scale)",
      y = NULL,
      color = "Outcome vs In-Person",
      shape = "Outcome vs In-Person",
      caption = caption
    ) +
    theme_publication()
}

7. EXPORT TABLE

make_or_table <- function(tidy_df, term_labels, drop_intercept = TRUE, digits = 2) {
  
  df <- tidy_df %>%
    { if (drop_intercept) filter(., term != "(Intercept)") else . } %>%
    mutate(
      term_label = dplyr::recode(term, !!!term_labels),
      or_cell = paste0(
        formatC(OR, format = "f", digits = digits),
        p_stars(p)
      ),
      ci_cell = paste0(
        "[", formatC(OR_lo, format = "f", digits = digits),
        ", ", formatC(OR_hi, format = "f", digits = digits), "]"
      ),
      se_cell = formatC(se, format = "f", digits = digits)
    )
  
  wide <- df %>%
    select(term_label, outcome, or_cell, se_cell, ci_cell) %>%
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = outcome,
      values_from = c(or_cell, se_cell, ci_cell),
      names_glue = "{outcome}_{.value}"
    )
  
  wide
}
fml_m1 <- arrangement ~ teleworkable + industry + income + age + education + employment + gender + race + household + vehicles + county_type + home_cbsa + year
m1_fit <- nnet::multinom(fml_m1, data = m1_dat, trace = FALSE)
V_cl <- vcov_multinom_cluster(m1_fit, m1_dat$county, type = "CR1")
df_t <- n_distinct(m1_dat$county) - 1
m1_tidy <- tidy_multinom_or(m1_fit, V_cl, df_t = df_t)
m1_stats <- m1_fit_tbl(m1_fit, m1_dat, "arrangement")
terms_m1 <- c(
  "teleworkableTeleworkable",
#  "office_settingOffice",
  "industryProfessional/Finance/Information",

)
plot_df_m1_full <- prep_or_plot(
  tidy_df = m1_tidy %>% filter(p < 0.10),
  terms_to_plot = terms_m1,
  term_labels = labels_m1,
  term_order = order_m1
)

p_m1_full <- plot_or(
  plot_df_m1_full,
  title = "Model 1A: Correlates of Work Arrangement",
  subtitle = "Odds Ratios with County-Clustered 95% CIs (Log Scale).\nReference: In-Person Worker. Full Sample (N = 2,550).",
  caption = caption_m1_full
)

p_m1_full

ggsave("results/model1_full_sample.png", p_m1_full, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)

FULL COEFFICIENT TABLE FOR APPENDIX

all_labels <- c(
  "desk_task" = "Desk Tasks",
  "interpersonal_task" = "Intpersonal Tasks",
  "physical_task" = "Physical Tasks",
  "industryProfessional/Finance/Information" = "Professional/Finance/Info",

  "county_typeOutlying" = "Outlying County",
  "home_cbsaWashington" = "Washington Metro",
  "home_cbsaOther" = "Other/Non-metro",
  "year2024" = "Year: 2024",
  "year2025" = "Year: 2025"
)

# Model 1 appendix table (teleworkable only)
table_m1 <- make_or_table(m1_tidy, all_labels)

# Model 1S appendix table (full sample)
all_labels_s <- c(
  "teleworkableTeleworkable" = "Teleworkable (Dingel-Neiman)",
  all_labels
)
table_m1s <- make_or_table(m1_tidy, all_labels_s)

table_m1s
## # A tibble: 27 × 7
##    term_label        Hybrid_or_cell Remote_or_cell Hybrid_se_cell Remote_se_cell
##    <chr>             <chr>          <chr>          <chr>          <chr>         
##  1 Teleworkable (Di… 3.11***        3.40***        0.12           0.11          
##  2 Professional/Fin… 2.42***        3.08***        0.18           0.19          
##  3 Health Care       1.14           1.49†          0.24           0.23          
##  4 Education         0.57**         0.41*          0.18           0.35          
##  5 Government        2.50***        1.25           0.20           0.32          
##  6 Construction/Mfg… 1.45           1.50†          0.24           0.21          
##  7 Other Industries  1.62*          1.40           0.20           0.43          
##  8 Income: <$50k     0.64*          0.82           0.20           0.21          
##  9 Income: $100-150k 0.98           1.10           0.23           0.13          
## 10 Income: >$150k    1.12           1.21           0.30           0.15          
## # ℹ 17 more rows
## # ℹ 2 more variables: Hybrid_ci_cell <chr>, Remote_ci_cell <chr>
wb <- openxlsx::createWorkbook()

openxlsx::addWorksheet(wb, "M1_Teleworkable_Table")
openxlsx::writeData(wb, "M1_Teleworkable_Table", table_m1)

openxlsx::addWorksheet(wb, "M1_Teleworkable_Fit")
openxlsx::writeData(wb, "M1_Teleworkable_Fit", m1_stats)

# openxlsx::addWorksheet(wb, "M1S_FullSample_Table")
# openxlsx::writeData(wb, "M1S_FullSample_Table", table_m1s)
# 
# openxlsx::addWorksheet(wb, "M1S_FullSample_Fit")
# openxlsx::writeData(wb, "M1S_FullSample_Fit", m1_stats)

openxlsx::saveWorkbook(
  wb,
  "results/Model_Results_Test.xlsx",
  overwrite = TRUE
)
